author: geunhwan
title: JS API E2E Electron cleanup fails on Windows - Permission denied removing locked electron.exe/dll
signature: rm cannot remove electron.exe Permission denied|Flaky Test

failed_jobs.name: JS API (22)
failed_jobs.symptom: Mocha after-all hook fails: rm -rf demo-electron-app-project — Permission denied on electron.exe and DLLs

key_errors:
exec error: Error: Command failed: rm -rf demo-electron-app-project
rm: cannot remove 'demo-electron-app-project/node_modules/electron/dist/d3dcompiler_47.dll': Permission denied
rm: cannot remove 'demo-electron-app-project/node_modules/electron/dist/electron.exe': Permission denied
rm: cannot remove 'demo-electron-app-project/node_modules/electron/dist/libEGL.dll': Permission denied
rm: cannot remove 'demo-electron-app-project/node_modules/electron/dist/libGLESv2.dll': Permission denied

1 failing
  1) E2E testing for OpenVINO as an Electron dependency.
       "after all" hook for "should run electron package and verify output":
     Error: Command failed: rm -rf demo-electron-app-project
root_cause: The two E2E tests passed successfully (2 passing). The failure occurs only in the Mocha 'after all' cleanup hook which attempts 'rm -rf demo-electron-app-project'. On Windows, the electron process (electron.exe) launched during the test is still running or its loaded DLLs (d3dcompiler_47.dll, libEGL.dll, libGLESv2.dll) are still locked by the OS at cleanup time, preventing deletion. This is a Windows file-locking issue unrelated to the PR changes (which revert GPU RMS normalization FP32 logic). The same pattern has been seen 4 previous times on Windows CI.
reproduction_steps: Run 'npm run test:e2e' in src/bindings/js/node on a Windows runner without waiting for the electron process to fully exit before cleanup.
recommended_actions: Add a delay or process-kill step before the rm -rf cleanup in electron-app.test.js to ensure electron.exe has fully exited
Use rimraf or a Windows-aware delete utility instead of bare rm -rf for cleanup on Windows
Add a retry loop or use 'robocopy /mir /e /purge' style cleanup that tolerates Windows file locks
Re-run the failed job — the underlying tests passed; only the cleanup hook is flaky
prevention_strategies: Fix the Mocha after-all hook in electron-app.test.js to explicitly terminate the electron process before attempting directory cleanup on Windows. Consider using a Windows-compatible file deletion library.
historical_context: This is the 5th occurrence of this same pattern. Previous runs: 29450212856 (2026-07-15), 29268143021 (2026-07-13), 29072781821 (2026-07-10), 29010970383 (2026-07-09). All on Windows CI JS API E2E tests.
